---
name: "LinkMerce Flat Punctuation"
description: "Use when writing or revising repository text, docs, comments, prompts, or instructions. Prefer flat ASCII punctuation and avoid curly quotes or decorative punctuation unless the source text explicitly requires them."
applyTo: "**/*"
---

# LinkMerce Flat Punctuation

## Punctuation rules

- Prefer ASCII punctuation in repository text.
- Use `"` for double quotes.
- Use `'` for single quotes.
- Prefer `-` over `–` or `—`.
- Avoid curly quotes like `“ ” ‘ ’`.
- Avoid decorative punctuation unless the source text explicitly requires it.
- Keep punctuation visually plain and consistent across docs, comments, and instruction files.

## Writing rules

- When rewriting text, normalize punctuation to the flat style unless doing so would change meaning.
- Keep existing technical identifiers, filenames, and code snippets unchanged.
- If a quote is part of a code sample or literal syntax, preserve the exact syntax required by the language or format.
- Do not introduce mixed quote styles in the same document unless quoting nested content.

## Scope

- Applies to documentation, comments, prompts, instruction files, skill files, and other repository text.
- Prefer this style even when the surrounding prose is informal.
